# Reproducible AI API stability evidence

> JSONL observations, success rate, P50/P95 latency and HTTP error distributions.

## Evidence rules

- Keep one raw request per JSONL line before summarizing.
- Record UTC time, exact model ID, region, network, status, elapsed time and request feature.
- Split text, streaming, tool-call and image workloads.
- Publish sample count, observation window, failure definition, P50/P95 and status distribution together.
- A smoke test is not a long-term availability result.

## Three distinct figures

- First-party product availability is an operator-published metric.
- Test-window success rate describes declared samples and conditions.
- Contractual SLA is defined by service terms and remedies.

These figures must not be substituted for one another. The bundled JSONL is synthetic format data, not AIFast production monitoring.

## Entry points

- Full methodology: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/AI-API-Stability-Tracker/blob/main/llms-full.txt
- Summarizer: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/AI-API-Stability-Tracker/blob/main/tools/summarize_results.py
- Sample JSONL: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/AI-API-Stability-Tracker/blob/main/examples/availability.sample.jsonl
- Model-check baseline: https://docs.aifast.club/model-check/
- AIFast claims and evidence: https://kkwang4444.github.io/api-status/evidence/
